name: Utah Dept of Technology Services (DTS)
tagline: Utah's consolidated central IT shop — DTS delivers enterprise infrastructure, security, and digital services to nearly every executive-branch agency, making it one of the most centralized state IT models in the country.
overview: The Utah Department of Technology Services (DTS) is the state's consolidated central IT agency and one of the most centralized models in the US — it provides enterprise technology services (infrastructure, networking, security, application development, and end-user support) to nearly all executive-branch agencies rather than letting each agency run its own IT shop. DTS is a division of the Department of Government Operations and operates under the State Chief Information Officer (CIO). Because consolidation is so deep, DTS is the buying center for most statewide technology decisions: agency CIOs and IT staff are embedded under DTS, so a seller's relationship with DTS reaches across the executive branch instead of one agency at a time. DTS is also visibly AI-forward, building enterprise AI infrastructure and governance for agencies in step with Utah's broader pro-human posture and the state's Office of Artificial Intelligence Policy (created by the 2024 AI Policy Act, SB-149). For a Dell SLG seller, DTS is the single highest-leverage relationship in Utah state government.

budget.intro: DTS is funded primarily as an internal service fund (ISF): it recovers costs by charging agencies rates for the services it delivers rather than drawing one large general-fund appropriation. The Legislature authorizes those rates each session, so the budget story is about the rate structure and the agency dollars flowing through it, not a single line item.

stats.label: Funding model
stats.note: Cost-recovery rates charged to agencies; legislatively authorized each session

stats.label: State budget (FY2027)
stats.note: All-sources budget from the 2026 General Session — the pool DTS-served agency IT draws from

stats.label: Rate direction
stats.note: DTS has moved toward consolidating rates into a user/device 'seat' model

notes: As an internal service fund, DTS bills participating agencies for compute, storage, network, security, and support — confirm the relevant rate category before forecasting a deal's funding path.
Annual rate authorization runs through the Legislature's State Agency Fees and Internal Service Fund Rate Authorization bill; check the current session's bill for the latest approved rates.

initiatives.name: Enterprise AI program
initiatives.summary: DTS is standing up an AI program — providing infrastructure, platforms, data governance, and ethics/standards so agencies can adopt generative AI safely. It is actively evaluating and rolling out gen-AI use cases across the executive branch, aligned with Utah's pro-human AI posture.

initiatives.name: Statewide IT consolidation
initiatives.summary: Deep consolidation is the defining DTS feature — enterprise infrastructure, networking, and shared services span nearly all executive-branch agencies. Modernization and refresh decisions are made centrally, which is where infrastructure and platform plays land.

initiatives.name: Cybersecurity & state Cyber Center
initiatives.summary: DTS runs statewide security under the CISO, including the Utah Cyber Center, setting security standards and operating SOC/incident-response capabilities for served agencies. Security requirements gate most infrastructure and cloud purchases.

initiatives.name: Cloud & digital services
initiatives.summary: DTS provides hybrid cloud, hosting, and digital/online-service delivery for agencies. Cloud and as-a-service offerings flow through DTS standards and statewide cooperative contracts rather than agency-by-agency buys.

procurement.intro: Technology buying in Utah runs through the Division of Purchasing and General Services on statewide and cooperative contracts, with DTS as the consolidated technical buyer/operator. Two access paths matter: holding the right state and cooperative contracts, and being positioned on DTS's enterprise standards and managed-services catalog.

vehicles.name: Utah Division of Purchasing statewide contracts
vehicles.detail: Central procurement authority for the state; pre-competed statewide cooperative contracts are the primary path for technology buys. The statewide contract search is the entry point for eligible entities.

vehicles.name: NASPO ValuePoint (Utah as lead state)
vehicles.detail: Utah's Division of Purchasing is an active NASPO ValuePoint lead state — including running Cloud and Software Solutions solicitations — giving Utah outsized influence over multi-state cooperative contracts that other states buy from.

vehicles.name: DTS enterprise / managed services
vehicles.detail: Because DTS is consolidated, much delivery flows through its rate-based managed services and enterprise standards. Aligning to DTS's catalog and architecture is the practical route into agency deployments.

powerStructure.name: Alan Fuller
powerStructure.title: State CIO (DTS)
powerStructure.note: Appointed State CIO in 2021; oversees IT for the executive branch through DTS. The top statewide technology relationship in Utah.

powerStructure.name: Phil Bates
powerStructure.title: State CISO
powerStructure.note: Long-tenured state CISO (since 2015); oversees the Utah Cyber Center and statewide security standards. Owns the security gate on most infrastructure/cloud deals.

powerStructure.name: DTS — under Dept of Government Operations
powerStructure.title: Consolidated central IT division
powerStructure.note: DTS sits within the Department of Government Operations; agency CIOs/IT staff are embedded under DTS, so the relationship reaches across the executive branch.

powerStructure.name: Utah Office of Artificial Intelligence Policy
powerStructure.title: AI policy & regulatory mitigation
powerStructure.note: Created by the 2024 AI Policy Act (SB-149), housed in the Dept of Commerce; shapes Utah's pro-human AI direction that DTS implements technically.

powerStructure.name: Division of Purchasing & General Services
powerStructure.title: Central procurement / NASPO lead state
powerStructure.note: Owns statewide contracts and Utah's NASPO ValuePoint lead-state solicitations — the contracting path for technology buys.

news.title: MyUtah statewide citizen portal keeps expanding services
news.impact: DTS's consolidated digital-services platform (myutah.gov, publicly launched Feb 2026) continues adding agency services — e.g. a live air-quality dashboard (June 30) with professional-license management rolling out. Signals sustained DTS investment in shared digital-experience infrastructure that Dell platform/data offerings can underpin.

news.title: NASPO ValuePoint Cloud & Software Solutions transitions to new 2026–2036 portfolio
news.impact: The legacy Utah-led cloud co-op contract expires Sept 15, 2026 and the new Cloud and Software Solutions (2026–2036) portfolio — awarded to ~58 suppliers with Utah as lead state — takes effect Sept 16, 2026. Confirm Dell/partner coverage on the new master agreements now; this vehicle shapes cloud/software buys well beyond Utah.

news.title: DTS reinforces GovRAMP cloud-security authorization gate for vendors
news.impact: DTS held GovRAMP office hours (May 2026) tied to cloud-security requirements in effect since July 1, 2025 — vendors selling cloud/SaaS into Utah increasingly need GovRAMP (formerly StateRAMP) authorization. A compliance gate to clear early on cloud offerings across the consolidated environment.

news.title: Utah names members of its Pro-Human AI Task Force
news.impact: Reinforces Utah's distinctive AI posture; DTS is the technical implementer of enterprise AI infrastructure and governance — a hook for AI-infrastructure conversations.

news.title: Utah launches first state-approved AI program for prescription renewals
news.impact: Signals Utah's willingness to deploy AI in production public services, validating demand for the underlying compute and data infrastructure DTS supports.

news.title: State CISO Phil Bates recognized among GovTech Top 25 (2026)
news.impact: Stable, high-profile security leadership at DTS — confirms the CISO as a durable relationship for the security buying center.

dellActions: Treat DTS as the single highest-leverage relationship in Utah — its deep consolidation means one DTS engagement reaches nearly the entire executive branch, not one agency.
Lead with AI infrastructure and ISG: tie compute, storage, and data-platform offerings to DTS's enterprise AI program and Utah's visible AI deployments.
Map deals to the internal-service-fund rate model — confirm which DTS rate category (and the current legislatively authorized rates) funds a given offering before forecasting.
Confirm Dell's coverage on Utah Division of Purchasing statewide contracts and relevant NASPO ValuePoint vehicles — and leverage Utah's NASPO lead-state role for cooperative-contract positioning.
Engage the CISO / Utah Cyber Center early — security standards gate most infrastructure and cloud purchases across the consolidated environment.
